name: ART – Restorative Streetscape Perception
description: Measures the restorative potential of street environments using Attention Restoration Theory (ART). Rates four dimensions: Being Away (mental escape), Fascination (effortless attention), Extent (coherent world), and Compatibility (match with personal goals). Based on Kaplan (1995).
author: Kaplan

config.title: Restorative Streetscape Perception Survey (ART)
config.description: Rate the restorative quality of street scenes using Attention Restoration Theory. Based on: Kaplan, S. (1995). The restorative benefits of nature: Toward an integrative framework. Journal of Environmental Psychology, 15(3), 169–182.

pages.name: page_intro
pages.title: About This Survey
pages.description: Attention Restoration Theory (ART) proposes that natural environments restore directed-attention capacity. This survey assesses whether street scenes possess restorative qualities across four dimensions: Being Away, Fascination, Extent, and Compatibility.

elements.name: art_intro
elements.title: Instructions
elements.description: For each street scene, you will be asked to rate four restorative qualities. Read each statement carefully and rate how much it applies to the scene shown, from 1 (Not at all) to 5 (Very much).

pages.name: page_art_1
pages.title: Restorative Quality Assessment
pages.description: Study the street scene below carefully before answering.

elements.name: art_dimensions_1
elements.title: Rate the following restorative dimensions for this scene (1 = Not at all, 5 = Very much):

rows.text: Being Away – This place feels like an escape from my everyday routine and mental demands
Fascination – This place captures my attention effortlessly and holds genuine interest
Extent (Scope) – This place feels like part of a larger, coherent environment to explore
Extent (Coherence) – This environment hangs together as a unified, ordered whole
Compatibility – This place supports the activities and purposes I would seek there

elements.name: overall_restoration_1
elements.title: Overall: how restorative does this street environment feel?

pages.name: page_art_2
pages.title: Restorative Quality Assessment
pages.description: Study the street scene below carefully before answering.

elements.name: art_dimensions_2
elements.title: Rate the following restorative dimensions for this scene (1 = Not at all, 5 = Very much):

rows.text: Being Away – This place feels like an escape from my everyday routine and mental demands
Fascination – This place captures my attention effortlessly and holds genuine interest
Extent (Scope) – This place feels like part of a larger, coherent environment to explore
Extent (Coherence) – This environment hangs together as a unified, ordered whole
Compatibility – This place supports the activities and purposes I would seek there

elements.name: overall_restoration_2
elements.title: Overall: how restorative does this street environment feel?

pages.name: page_pairwise_restoration
pages.title: Comparative Restoration
pages.description: Choose the scene that would be more mentally restorative.

elements.name: restoration_pairwise
elements.title: Which street scene would be more mentally restorative / refreshing?

elements.name: nature_best
elements.title: Which scene provides the greatest connection with nature?

pages.name: page_mental_health_context
pages.title: Personal Context (Optional)
pages.description: These questions help us understand how individual context influences restorative perception.

elements.name: stress_level
elements.title: How would you describe your current stress level?

elements.name: nature_access
elements.title: How easy is it for you to access green or natural spaces near your home?

elements.name: restoration_comments
elements.title: What features of street environments do you find most mentally restorative? (Optional)
